stage.name: Checkpoint 06: from execution result to architectural state
stage.description: A choice-only checkpoint for classifying EX_STAGE units, branch resolution, commit discipline, CSR/store side effects, and precise exceptions.
stage.courseTemplateName: Professional Technical Deep Dive - Evidence-Grounded SlideKit

scenes.title: Checkpoint: execute, commit, and precise state

content.title: Checkpoint 06: from execution result to architectural state
content.subtitle: This checkpoint tests whether you can classify backend events without mixing execute work and commit authority.
content.thesis: The goal is simple: when you see a backend event, decide whether it is compute, control repair, buffered side effect, architectural update, or precise trap handling.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: EX_STAGE module
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: EX_STAGE is the logical execute stage and encapsulates ALU, Branch Unit, CSR buffer, Mult, load/store, and CVXIF.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: COMMIT_STAGE module
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: COMMIT_STAGE is the last pipeline stage and updates architectural state by writing CSR registers, committing stores, and writing back data to the register file.

content.speakerNotes: This checkpoint is where we see whether Unit 06 has become usable knowledge. I am not asking you to memorize every signal. I want you to classify events. Is this computation? Branch correction? Buffered side-effect intent? Ordered architectural update? Precise trap handling? That classification is what makes a large backend readable.

actions.text: This checkpoint is where we see whether Unit 06 has become usable knowledge. I am not asking you to memorize every signal. I want you to classify events. Is this computation? Branch correction? Buffered side-effect intent? Ordered architectural update? Precise trap handling? That classification is what makes a large backend readable.

content.title: Every case should land somewhere on this path
content.body: Issue admits and tracks work. Execute computes, resolves, or buffers. Commit updates architectural state and converges precise trap behavior.
content.caption: The checkpoint uses the same architectural map, but now as a diagnostic tool.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: EX_STAGE module
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: EX_STAGE is the logical execute stage and encapsulates ALU, Branch Unit, CSR buffer, Mult, load/store, and CVXIF.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: COMMIT_STAGE module
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: COMMIT_STAGE is the last pipeline stage and updates architectural state by writing CSR registers, committing stores, and writing back data to the register file.

content.speakerNotes: Use the architecture map as a diagnostic tool. A case either belongs to issue tracking, execute work, result return, commit, or control repair. If a statement says the ALU commits stores, it is wrong. If it says branch prediction is final branch reality, it is wrong. If it says CSR side effects wait for commit discipline, now we are in the right neighborhood.

actions.text: Use the architecture map as a diagnostic tool. A case either belongs to issue tracking, execute work, result return, commit, or control repair. If a statement says the ALU commits stores, it is wrong. If it says branch prediction is final branch reality, it is wrong. If it says CSR side effects wait for commit discipline, now we are in the right neighborhood.

content.title: Label the event before explaining the signal
content.subtitle: This keeps a short lesson technical instead of vague.

cards.title: Compute
cards.body: ALU arithmetic or multiplier work that produces a result.

cards.title: Resolve
cards.body: Branch Unit decides outcome, target, and correction.

cards.title: Buffer
cards.body: CSR or store intent waits until commit authority.

cards.title: Retire
cards.body: Commit acknowledges and updates architectural state.

cards.title: Trap
cards.body: Precise exception or interrupt handling records context and redirects.

content.takeaway: Classification first, signal detail second. This is how the course stays understandable.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: EX_STAGE module
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: EX_STAGE is the logical execute stage and encapsulates ALU, Branch Unit, CSR buffer, Mult, load/store, and CVXIF.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: Branch Unit submodule
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The Branch Unit manages conditional and unconditional jumps, calculates targets, decides take or not take, and reports corrective actions.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: COMMIT_STAGE module
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: COMMIT_STAGE is the last pipeline stage and updates architectural state by writing CSR registers, committing stores, and writing back data to the register file.

sourceAnchors.title: CVA6 user manual: Traps, Interrupts and Exceptions
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The user manual distinguishes synchronous exceptions from asynchronous interrupts and lists trap-related CSR state such as mtvec, mepc, mcause, and mtval.

content.speakerNotes: These five labels are the checkpoint vocabulary. Compute, resolve, buffer, retire, trap. They are intentionally plain. If a student can label the case correctly first, the signal details have somewhere to attach. If they cannot, adding more RTL names just makes the misunderstanding harder to notice.

actions.text: These five labels are the checkpoint vocabulary. Compute, resolve, buffer, retire, trap. They are intentionally plain. If a student can label the case correctly first, the signal details have somewhere to attach. If they cannot, adding more RTL names just makes the misunderstanding harder to notice.

questions.question: A branch was predicted taken, but the real branch outcome in execute says it was not taken. What is the best label?
questions.analysis: The Branch Unit resolves the real outcome and can provide corrective information.

questions.question: A CSR instruction carries an operation that should not update CSR state until the instruction is allowed to retire. What is the best label?
questions.analysis: The CSR buffer exists because CSR side effects need commit discipline.

questions.question: Which statements follow the Unit 06 architectural-state rule?

options.label: Execution may produce a result before commit
Commit is where architectural updates become official
Wrong-path stores should freely update memory
Precise exception handling depends on ordered state boundaries

questions.analysis: Wrong-path stores must not freely update architectural state.

questions.question: Which unit is most directly responsible for deciding taken versus not taken after a branch reaches execute?
questions.analysis: The Branch Unit manages conditional branch decisions and target behavior.

questions.question: Why does precise exception handling naturally connect to commit?

options.label: Commit is the architectural boundary where ordered state updates are controlled
Commit predicts all future branch targets
Commit is unrelated to instruction order

questions.analysis: Precise handling needs ordered association with the right instruction and state boundary.

questions.question: Which items should appear in a serious public explanation of a trap?

options.label: Whether it is an exception or interrupt
The architectural boundary or related instruction
Relevant trap CSR context such as EPC or cause
A claim that early detection always immediately changes state

questions.analysis: Early detection must still respect the ordered architectural boundary.

sourceAnchors.title: CVA6 user manual: Traps, Interrupts and Exceptions
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The user manual distinguishes synchronous exceptions from asynchronous interrupts and lists trap-related CSR state such as mtvec, mepc, mcause, and mtval.

content.speakerNotes: This checkpoint is deliberately choice-only so it can work offline and still give useful feedback. The right answers show the same pattern again and again: compute does not equal commit, prediction does not equal resolution, and a trap is not just an error signal. It is ordered architectural control.

actions.text: This checkpoint is deliberately choice-only so it can work offline and still give useful feedback. The right answers show the same pattern again and again: compute does not equal commit, prediction does not equal resolution, and a trap is not just an error signal. It is ordered architectural control.

content.title: Now memory-system lessons have a clean landing zone
content.subtitle: LSU, cache, MMU, and store ordering make more sense after commit discipline is clear.

columns.title: What is stable now
columns.body: You can separate execute work, branch repair, state buffering, retirement, and precise trap handling.

columns.title: What comes next
columns.body: Memory lessons can now explain loads, stores, LSU queues, TLB, PTW, and cache behavior without confusing side effects.

content.takeaway: The memory system is easier when execute and commit are no longer vague words.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: EX_STAGE module
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: EX_STAGE is the logical execute stage and encapsulates ALU, Branch Unit, CSR buffer, Mult, load/store, and CVXIF.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: COMMIT_STAGE module
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: COMMIT_STAGE is the last pipeline stage and updates architectural state by writing CSR registers, committing stores, and writing back data to the register file.

content.speakerNotes: Unit 06 unlocks the next major topic. Memory behavior is not just address plus data. Loads return values, stores create side effects, translation can fault, and cache behavior needs ordering context. Because we now have execute and commit separated, Unit 07 can discuss LSU, MMU, TLB, PTW, and cache behavior with much less confusion.

actions.text: Unit 06 unlocks the next major topic. Memory behavior is not just address plus data. Loads return values, stores create side effects, translation can fault, and cache behavior needs ordering context. Because we now have execute and commit separated, Unit 07 can discuss LSU, MMU, TLB, PTW, and cache behavior with much less confusion.

content.title: You can now classify backend events
takeaways: EX_STAGE contains units with distinct contracts, not one generic execution box.
Branch resolution checks prediction against execute-stage reality.
COMMIT_STAGE is the architectural-state boundary for writes, stores, CSR, and precise traps.
content.nextStep: Unit 07: Memory, LSU, cache, MMU, TLB, and page-table-walk reading discipline.
content.closing: The backend is no longer a blur. It is a sequence of contracts.

sourceAnchors.claimSupported: COMMIT_STAGE is the last pipeline stage and updates architectural state by writing CSR registers, committing stores, and writing back data to the register file.

content.speakerNotes: Unit 06 is complete. You can now classify backend events instead of guessing from stage names. EX_STAGE contains distinct contracts. Branch resolution checks frontend optimism against real evidence. COMMIT_STAGE is the architectural-state boundary. Next, we use this discipline to enter memory: loads, stores, cache, MMU, TLB, and page-table walks.
